title: Office supervisors
description: Advise and educate individuals or organizations on acquiring and managing debt. May provide guidance in determining the best type of loan and explain loan requirements or restrictions. May help develop debt management plans or student financial aid packages. May advise on credit issues, or provide budget, mortgage, bankruptcy, or student financial aid counseling. Evaluate, authorize, or recommend approval of commercial, real estate, or credit loans. Advise borrowers on financial status and payment methods. Includes mortgage loan officers and agents, collection analysts, loan servicing officers, loan underwriters, and payday loan officers. Compile, process, and maintain medical records of hospital and clinic patients in a manner consistent with medical, administrative, ethical, legal, and regulatory requirements of the healthcare system. Classify medical and healthcare concepts, including diagnosis, procedures, medical services, and equipment, into the healthcare industry's numerical coding system. Includes medical coders. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research and provide clients with guidance on acquiring and managing debt, including developing debt management plans and advising on loan types, eligibility requirements, and repayment strategies. Evaluate clients' financial situations and recommend financial strategies to meet their goals.
Negotiate with creditors on behalf of clients to arrange payment adjustments or plans, and discuss housing matters such as mortgage delinquency and foreclosure prevention.
Explain financial topics to clients, including budgeting, credit report ratings, and consumer protection laws, and refer clients to relevant community resources for comprehensive support.
Maintain records of client activity, including financial transactions and correspondence, and prepare written documents establishing financial recommendations or contracts with clients.
Assist clients with personal financial issues such as excessive spending or borrowing and contact clients with delinquent accounts to negotiate repayment plans.
Create action plans for clients to help them secure permanent housing through rent or mortgage programs and assess changes in their financial situations to update debt management plans.
Provide guidance and support to office personnel, ensuring quality standards and adherence to company policies, and evaluate employees' performance while making recommendations for improvements.
Train employees on job duties and company policies, and implement training and development programs. Supervise and coordinate activities among office staff to ensure efficiency and productivity.
Perform general office duties, including filing, answering phones, and handling correspondence, as well as maintaining inventory and office service operations.
Evaluate financial activities, assist in budget planning, and ensure compliance with relevant regulations and procedures in financial transactions and operations.
Compile and prepare various reports and statistical analyses based on financial data to assist management, while ensuring accuracy in records and financial calculations.
Monitor loan status and account payments, ensuring discrepancies are reported and resolved, and manage records according to established policies and procedures.
Prepare and issue financial documents, calculate, and prepare payments as needed, including bills, invoices, and account statements, while ensuring accuracy and compliance.
Analyze applicants' credit and financial statements to assess eligibility for loans and evaluate loan agreements for accuracy according to company policy.
Supervise various administrative operations and staff, ensuring adherence to productivity standards, scheduling, and assignment of duties to meet organizational goals.
Address and resolve customer complaints and inquiries regarding policies and procedures, providing quality customer support.

detailed_work_activities.title: Perform clerical work in medical settings.
Educate clients on financial planning topics.
Verify accuracy of financial or transactional data.
Correspond with customers to answer questions or resolve complaints.
Collect medical information from patients, family members, or other medical professionals.
Communicate with management or other staff to resolve problems.
Classify materials according to standard systems.
Interview clients to gather financial information.
Maintain data in information systems or databases.
Monitor medical facility activities to ensure adherence to standards or regulations.
Reconcile records of sales or other financial transactions.
Enter patient or treatment data into computers.
Prepare official health documents or records.
Prepare documentation for contracts, transactions, or regulatory compliance.
Verify application data to determine program eligibility.
Prepare financial documents, reports, or budgets.
Prepare contracts or other transaction documents.
Confer with others about financial matters.
Inform individuals or organizations of status or findings.
Refer clients to community or social service programs.
Recommend products or services to customers.
Disburse funds from clients accounts to creditors.
Search files, databases or reference materials to obtain needed information.
Prepare cash for deposit or disbursement.
Calculate costs of goods or services.
Convert data among multiple digital or analog formats.
Answer telephones to direct calls or provide information.
Execute sales or other financial transactions.
Examine documents to verify adherence to requirements.
Maintain current knowledge related to work activities.
Confer with coworkers to coordinate work activities.
Report maintenance or equipment problems to appropriate personnel.
Respond to customer problems or complaints.
Administer personnel recruitment or hiring activities.
Monitor inventories of products or materials.
Gather medical information from patient histories.
Monitor external affairs or events affecting business operations.
Evaluate applicable laws and regulations to determine impact on organizational activities.
Create databases to store electronic data.
Train caregivers or other non-medical personnel.
Recommend changes to improve computer or information systems.
